tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Pull the full ticket record including subject, body, submitter email, priority, and timestamps. Always call this first.

tools.name: lookup_customer_info
tools.description: Find a customer account by email address or customer ID. Use the submitter email from the ticket when you do not yet have a customer ID.

identifier.description: Email address (e.g. user@company.com) or customer ID (e.g. cust_8fK2mQ)

tools.name: get_customer_profile
tools.description: Retrieve the full customer profile: plan tier, MRR, seat count, billing contact, SSO status, and usage history. Call this after lookup_customer_info gives you the customer_id. Use this instead of any other customer detail tool.

customer_id.description: Customer ID from lookup_customer_info, e.g. cust_8fK2mQ

lookback_days.description: Days of usage history to include. Default: 30. Use 90 for billing disputes or trend analysis.

tools.name: search_prior_tickets
tools.description: Search the customer's ticket history by keyword. Use this to find related open issues, check if this is a repeat problem, or review what the customer has reported before.

tools.name: check_entitlements
tools.description: Check what features and limits the customer's plan includes. Use before telling a customer that something is or is not available on their plan.

feature.description: Optional: a specific feature to check, e.g. 'sso', 'api_rate_limit', 'seats'

tools.name: check_system_status
tools.description: Check real-time health of Meridian platform components. Use when the ticket describes widespread errors or outages that may be on our side.

tools.name: spawn_specialist
tools.description: Spawn a specialist subagent to investigate and resolve an issue. The specialist has deep domain tools and will do the resolution work. For tickets with multiple distinct issue categories, call spawn_specialist once per category — do not skip a category because another specialist is handling a different issue.

category.description: billing = invoices/refunds/plan changes. technical = API errors/webhooks/integrations. account = SSO/seats/permissions.

tools.name: write_response
tools.description: Send the final customer-facing response and set the ticket status. Call this once, after all specialists have returned and you have synthesized their findings. The response_body must address every issue raised in the ticket.

response_body.description: The complete customer-facing message. Must cover every distinct issue in the ticket.

resolution_status.description: resolved = all issues fully handled. escalated = handed to human. needs_info = waiting on customer reply.

tools.name: escalate_to_human
tools.description: Hand the ticket to a human agent. Use only when: the fix requires a code change, there is a security incident, the customer explicitly requested human support, or you have exhausted available tools without resolution.

summary_for_human.description: What was investigated, what was found, and what the human agent needs to do next.
